steps.title: Spot the contrasting philosophies
steps.content: Realism emphasizes accuracy and ordinary people; romanticism (marked by “instead”) accentuates positive traits and idealizes with embellishments. These are opposed approaches.

steps.title: Choose the oppositional verb
steps.content:
The blank needs a verb meaning to refuse, oppose, or move away from the romantic style — the realist style is described as opposite ("instead") to romanticism. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — rejected: "This style largely rejected the conventions of the romantic style" — rejected means refused or dismissed, exactly capturing the realist style's opposition to romanticism's idealization. ✓
  • Choice B — imitated: "largely imitated the conventions" — imitated means copied or followed; but the passage emphasizes the styles are opposed, not aligned. ✗
  • Choice C — epitomized: "largely epitomized the conventions" — epitomized means served as the perfect example of; realism cannot be the perfect example of romanticism if they're contrasting approaches. ✗
  • Choice D — extended: "largely extended the conventions" — extended means built on or expanded; this would mean realism continued romantic conventions, contradicting the "instead" contrast. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
